title: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
author: James Silberman
orientation: A misdirected letter from National Right to Life VP Tony Lauinger reveals a coordinated procedural strategy to block Oklahoma's SB13 abolition bill and prevent any related amendment from reaching the floor. The article uses the leaked memo to argue that establishment pro-life leaders are deliberately stringing voters along to preserve their political and financial power.

questions.question: How did the abolitionist movement obtain Lauinger's internal strategy letter?
questions.answer: The letter was misaddressed by Lauinger's office and sent to the wrong senator, who then forwarded it to Free the States, exposing the procedural plan to kill SB13.
questions.quote: A letter from Lauinger sent to the wrong Senator and then forwarded to the abolitionist organization Free the States details Lauinger's plan to prevent any consideration of abolishing abortion.

questions.question: What specific procedural maneuver did Lauinger suggest to prevent SB13 from being attached to another bill?
questions.answer: Lauinger recommended moving SB614 from the Health and Human Services Committee, where SB13 author Sen. Joseph Silk sits, over to the Judiciary Committee where Silk has no seat and could not introduce an abolition amendment.
questions.quote: "[SB614] could be moved from Health and Human Services to Judiciary, in which Sen. Silk does not sit," Lauinger wrote.

questions.question: What parliamentary objections did Lauinger propose using against an abolition amendment?
questions.answer: He proposed ruling such an amendment out of order under the single-subject rule, arguing it was not germane because it touched many titles of law, or simply tabling any motion to amend.
questions.quote: "An amendment regarding SB13 could be ruled out of order: as violating the single-subject rule (as it involves charges in many different titles of law); as being not germane (for the same reason – many different titles of law); [or] a motion to amend could be tabled. "

questions.question: Why does Lauinger insist the legislature keep hearing other pro-life bills even while blocking SB13?
questions.answer: He argues that refusing to hear any abortion-related bills would freeze the pro-life movement's momentum, particularly the Rose Day bills already moving through the House.
questions.quote: "Finally, Julie, refraining from hearing any abortion-related bills would paralyze the pro-life cause and make it impossible to make any progress. There were two other Rose Day abortion bills moving fast in the House."

questions.question: Why does the article dismiss the sex-selective abortion ban (HB1396) as ineffective?
questions.answer: Because the law is trivially circumvented: a mother need only state any reason other than the baby's sex for the abortion, so no abortions are actually prevented.
questions.quote: Bans on sex-selective abortions do nothing to prevent abortion. All the mother has to do is claim a reason for the abortion other than her baby's sex.

questions.question: According to the author, what hidden purposes do these incremental pro-life bills serve?
questions.answer: They generate fundraising material for Oklahomans for Life, give Republican legislators talking points for pro-life voters, delay actual abolition so the pro-life industry retains power, and distract the public from the tabling of SB13.
questions.quote: Delays the abolition of abortion so the pro-life industry and Republican establishment can maintain their political and financial power in perpetuity

questions.question: What action does the article ask Oklahoma constituents to take in response to this revelation?
questions.answer: It urges readers to directly pressure their state representatives and senators to demand abolition rather than incremental measures, warning that without that pressure the killing will continue.
questions.quote: Make sure your State Representatives and Senators know we demand abortion's abolition and nothing less. If we don't, the killing is not going to end.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Directly referenced in this article as prior reporting on Lauinger and his Senate allies blocking SB13.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Companion piece on the broader political and religious establishment opposition to SB13 in the same legislative session.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Continues the same March 2019 narrative about Senate leadership maneuvering to deny SB13 a hearing.

related.title: "Mom, I saved someone from Rose Day!"
related.reason: Expands on the Rose Day events and Tony Lauinger that this article uses as the lever for keeping incremental bills alive.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13" ?
related.reason: Lays out the abolitionist rationale for refusing the incremental pro-life bills Lauinger insists must keep moving.

related.title: "I am a pro-life Oklahoman who voted to table a bill that would abolish all abortion"
related.reason: Follow-up on Sen. Julie Daniels, the apparent recipient of Lauinger's strategy memo, after she tabled SB13.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution Demanding an Immediate "End" to Abortion
related.reason: Shows the church-side response to the pro-life industry tactics this article exposes.
